note:
众议员亚历山德里亚·奥卡西奥-科尔特斯(纽约州民主党人)今晚首次亮相Twitch，在我们中间发挥作用，鼓励观众投票。她也很快成为了该平台最大的广播商之一：根据Twitch的数据，她的流媒体在第一场比赛前后达到了43.5万观众的峰值。(截至撰写本文时，该流仍在播放，但在播放了大约两个小时后，它的收视率已降至约35万人。)。
根据第三方指标网站TwitchTracker的数据，这样的峰值收视率使她的广播跻身于有史以来最大的20个流媒体之列，如果你只看单个流媒体的广播，这个数字要高得多。忍者保持着个人流媒体的记录，在2018年与堡垒之夜的一场比赛中，有60多万观众观看了德雷克的比赛。TwitchTracker的衡量标准表明，就峰值观众而言，AOC的流媒体可能跻身个人前10名。
政客们越来越多地使用科技和游戏来传播他们的信息。拜登的竞选团队上周首次亮相了一个动物穿越岛(Animal Crossing Island)。去年，参议员伯尼·桑德斯(I-VT)加入了Twitch，以接触到“潜在的支持性观众，我们可能不会以其他方式打击他们。”
奥卡西奥-科尔特斯的溪流很快汇聚在一起。周一，她在推特上问道，“有人想在Twitch上和我一起玩，以获得投票吗？”主要的流媒体很快就签约了-她最终加入了众议员伊尔汗·奥马尔(D-MN)，波基马内，HasanAbi，伪装吐司，DrLupo等等。她的流媒体甚至准备了图片，Ocasio-Cortez说这些图片来自她发推文后开始创作艺术的支持者。
尽管在我们中间只有很少的经验-Ocasio-Cortez周一表示，她以前从未玩过，但似乎在流媒体之前温习了一下-她在第一次广播中做得很好。她在第一轮中被选为冒名顶替者，并与一名合作伙伴在被抓之前击倒了大约一半的场地。奥马尔后来以冒名顶替者的身份进入了三强，然后被奥卡西奥-科尔特斯和哈桑投票淘汰。
note_en:
Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-NY)  made her Twitch debut tonight to play  Among Us and encourage viewers to vote. She also, very quickly, became one of the platform’s biggest broadcasters: her stream peaked at 435,000 viewers around the time of her first match, according to Twitch. (The stream is still going as of this writing, but it had dipped to around 350,000 viewers after about two hours of playing.)
 That peak viewership puts her broadcast among the 20 biggest streams ever, according to the third-party metrics site  TwitchTracker, and much higher if you’re only looking at broadcasts from individual streamers. Ninja holds the record for an individual streamer, with more than 600,000 viewers  during a  Fortnite match with Drake in 2018. TwitchTracker’s metrics suggest that AOC’s stream could in the top 10 for an individual in terms of peak viewers.
 Politicians have increasingly been using tech and games to get out their message. The Biden campaign  debuted an  Animal Crossing island last week. Last year, Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-VT)  joined Twitch to reach a “potentially supportive audience that we may not be hitting other ways.”
   Ocasio-Cortez’s stream  came together quickly. She tweeted Monday asking, “Anyone want to play Among Us with me on Twitch to get out the vote?”  Major streamers quickly signed up — she ended up being joined by Rep. Ilhan Omar (D-MN), Pokimane, HasanAbi, Disguised Toast, DrLupo, and more. Her stream even had graphics prepared, which Ocasio-Cortez said came from supporters who started making art after she tweeted.
 Despite only having minimal  Among Us experience — Ocasio-Cortez said Monday that she’d never played before, but seemed to have brushed up before the stream — she did well in her first broadcast. She was chosen as an imposter in the first round and, with a partner, knocked out about half the field before getting caught. Omar later made it to the final three as an imposter before getting voted out by Ocasio-Cortez and Hasan.
title: AOC的首次Twitch Stream是有史以来最大的Twitch Stream之一
title_en: AOC’s debut Twitch stream is one of the biggest ever

note:
周二下午，People Netflix首席运营官格雷格·彼得斯在公司2020年第三季度财报电话会议上证实，Netflix希望通过48小时的免费流媒体活动来推广其服务。Netflix希望首先在印度测试免费流媒体服务，并可能在接下来的几个月里将其引入其他国家。
彼得斯说：我们认为，让一个国家的每个人都能在周末免费使用Netflix，这可能是让一群新人接触到我们拥有的令人惊叹的故事的一个很好的方式。真的要搞一场活动，希望能有一大群人报名参加。
周二早些时候，Protocol率先报道称，该公司的Android应用程序的最新版本提到了一项名为StreamFest的促销活动，该活动定于12月4日举行(向米沙尔·拉赫曼致敬)。Netflix的一位发言人周二上午确认了此次活动的计划，他告诉“礼宾”：我们一直在寻找不同的营销促销活动，以吸引新会员，并给他们带来很棒的Netflix体验。
如果按照应用程序代码中的调侃，StreamFest将可以通过任何目前可以使用Netflix的设备访问，而且用户不需要输入信用卡号码就可以访问免费流媒体。根据该应用程序中的一个文本字符串，我们不需要任何付款细节--只需要你的爱和一心一意的关注。然而，任何想要在为期两天的活动结束后继续观看的人都必须注册一个常规账户。
可以访问促销活动的人数似乎有一定的限制，因为应用程序内的一个文本字符串通知用户Netflix StreamFest已经满负荷。&#34；Netflix经常针对其部分受众测试新功能；限制访问权限可能是针对其他形式的促销活动测试StreamFest指标的一种方式。
Netflix计划推出StreamFest Surface，就在几天前，该公司刚刚停止在美国的30天免费试用。此次活动与HBO和Showtime等付费电视网多年来一直在推广其节目的方式相似。例如，Showtime在8月份向康卡斯特Xfinity有线电视订户免费开放了48小时。
Netflix第三季度的业绩低于该公司本已谨慎的预测。在本季度，Netflix在全球增加了220万付费用户，其中北美增加了不到20万。高管们将这在很大程度上归因于该公司在COVID成立头几个月的超高速增长，并表示Netflix在2020年前9个月增加的订户已经超过2019年全年。
疫情还影响了Netflix的生产计划，但联合首席执行官泰德·萨兰多斯(Ted Sarandos)表示，该公司将按计划在今年年底前完成150部作品。萨兰多斯说：“我们在实物生产方面回到了接近稳定的状态。”实质上，我们重新开业了。
title: Netflix计划在印度测试一个48小时免费的周末，名为StreamFest，定于12月4日举行，稍后可能会将促销活动带到其他国家
title_en: Netflix plans to test a 48-hour free weekend in India, dubbed StreamFest and scheduled for Dec. 4, and may bring the promotion to other countries later

note:
旧金山-10月21日星期三，美国东部时间上午11点/太平洋时间下午2点，选民倡导组织将要求地区法院阻止一项违宪的行政命令，该命令报复在线服务，因为在线服务核查特朗普总统关于投票和即将到来的选举的虚假帖子。有关出席视频庭审的信息可以在法院的网站上找到。
原告-共同事业、美国自由新闻、Maplight、Rock the Vote和Voto拉丁裔-由电子前沿基金会(EFF)、保护民主组织和Cooley LLP代表。在周三的听证会上，库利合伙人凯瑟琳·哈特尼特(Kathleen Hartnett)将辩称，在诉讼解决之前，总统的行政命令不应该得到执行。
特朗普在与推特进行了一场广为人知的斗争后，于5月份签署了《关于防止网络审查的行政令》。首先，总统在推特上发布了关于在线投票可靠性的虚假声明，然后推特决定在推特上附加一个链接，以“获取邮寄选票的事实”。两天后，特朗普签署了这项命令，指示政府机构开始对在线服务采取执法行动，因为任何所谓的温和“欺骗性行为”都不是“真诚的”。该命令还指示政府停止通过“不守信用”的社交媒体公司投放广告，并启动一项程序，以破坏第230条规定的平台的法律保护。第230条是一项法律，允许在线服务-如Twitter、Facebook和其他网站-主持和协调不同的用户言论论坛，而不对用户的内容负责。
本案的原告提起诉讼是因为他们希望确保网上发现的投票信息是准确的，他们希望社交媒体公司能够对错误信息采取积极措施。相反，这项行政命令禁止社交媒体公司以他不喜欢的方式缓和总统的内容-这是违反第一修正案的违宪行为。
note_en:
San Francisco – On Wednesday, October 21 at 11 am ET/2 pm PT, voter advocacy organizations will ask a district court to block an unconstitutional Executive Order that retaliates against online services for fact-checking President Trump’s false posts about voting and the upcoming election. Information on attending the video hearing can be found on the  court’s website.
 The plaintiffs—  Common Cause,  Free Press,  Maplight,  Rock the Vote, and  Voto Latino—are represented by the Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF),  Protect Democracy, and  Cooley LLP. At Wednesday’s hearing, Cooley partner Kathleen Hartnett will argue that the president’s Executive Order should not be enforced until the lawsuit is resolved.
 Trump signed the “ Executive Order on Preventing Online Censorship” in May, after a well-publicized fight with Twitter. First, the president tweeted false claims about the reliability of online voting, and then Twitter decided to append a link to “get the facts about mail-in ballots.” Two days later, Trump signed the order, which directs government agencies to begin law enforcement actions against online services for any supposedly “deceptive acts” of moderation that aren’t in “good faith.” The order also instructs the government to withhold advertising through social media companies who act in “bad faith,” and to kickstart a process to gut platforms’ legal protections under Section 230. Section 230 is the law that allows online services—like Twitter, Facebook, and others—to host and moderate diverse forums of users’ speech without being liable for their users’ content.
 The plaintiffs in this case filed the  lawsuit because they want to make sure that voting information found online is accurate, and they want social media companies to be able to take proactive steps against misinformation.  Instead, the Executive Order chills social media companies from moderating the president’s content in a way that he doesn’t like—an unconstitutional violation of the First Amendment.
title: 周三视频听证会：倡导组织诉诸法庭，阻止特朗普对事实核查的报复
title_en: Video Hearing Wednesday: Advocacy Orgs Go to Court to Block Trump’s Retaliation Against Fact-Checking

EVISIT是一家软件即服务(Saas)远程医疗公司，为医院和医疗系统提供虚拟护理，该公司宣布了1400万美元的首轮融资。专注于软件的成长型股票基金TVC Capital牵头融资。
EVISIT的虚拟医疗平台为100多家医院和医疗系统提供服务，其中包括美国一些最大的医疗企业。该平台支持数十万次虚拟访问，并在符合HIPAA的平台内连接提供者和患者。这使卫生保健提供者能够提供更高效的病人护理。
这笔资金将进一步推动eVISIT在远程医疗行业的快速扩张，在这个远程医疗的关键时期，在不断变化的新冠肺炎格局中，为我们的技术平台和客户服务活动提供更多的资源，“eVISIT的联合创始人兼首席执行官布雷特·拉森说。
拉森接着说：“除了用杰出的人才扩展我们的组织外，我们还计划进一步加强我们的营销和销售拓展，以支持我们专注于进入市场的战略。新一轮谈判证明了来自这些合作伙伴的集体信心，他们相信我们有能力推动整个北美的医院和卫生系统的虚拟护理战略和执行。
EVisit的虚拟护理平台为所有专业提供灵活的虚拟工作流程。其端到端技术将医疗流程中的每个步骤虚拟化，包括日程安排、入院和候诊室管理、虚拟探视以及支付和出院。
EVITE正在与全国的医疗保健系统和提供者团体合作，以支持虚拟医疗并简化医疗服务的提供。
我们非常高兴有机会与eVITE的这支令人惊叹的团队合作，“TVC Capital的管理合伙人史蒂夫·哈默斯拉格(Steve Hamergrag)说。
“随着供应商和终端用户对这一关键功能的快速采用，远程医疗市场正在爆炸式增长。这一企业级服务的深度给我们留下了非常深刻的印象，他们庞大而成熟的客户群采用的广度证明了这一点。EVITE在这个不断扩大的市场中占有较早的位置，我们很高兴能成为帮助改善患者结果，同时降低成本的旅程的一部分。
note_en:
eVisit, a  software as a service (Saas)  telehealth company that enables virtual care for hospitals and health systems, announced $14 million in Series A funding. TVC Capital, a software-focused growth equity fund, led the funding.
  eVisit’s Virtual Care platform serves over 100 hospitals and health systems, including some of the largest  health care enterprises in the U.S. The platform supports hundreds of thousands of virtual visits and connects providers and patients within the HIPAA-compliant platform. This enables health care providers to deliver more efficient patient care.
 "This funding will further eVisit's rapid expansion within the telehealth industry by providing greater resources to our technology platform and customer care activities during this pivotal time for telehealth, in the ever-changing COVID-19 landscape and beyond,” said Bret Larsen, co-founder and CEO of eVisit.
 Larsen continued, “In addition to scaling our organization with exceptional talent, we are planning to further enhance our marketing and sales outreach to support our focused go-to-market strategy. This new round is a testament to the collective confidence from these partners in our ability to power the virtual care strategies and execution for hospitals and health systems across North America."
 eVisit's Virtual Care platform offers flexible virtual workflows for all specialties. Its end-to-end technology  virtualizes each step in the health care process, including scheduling, intake and waiting room management, virtual visits, and payment and discharge.
   eVisit is working with health care systems and provider groups nationwide to support virtual care and simplify health care delivery.
 "We are very pleased to have the opportunity to work with this amazing team at eVisit,” said Steve Hamerslag, managing partner at TVC Capital.
 “The telehealth market is exploding with rapid provider and end user adoption of this critical capability. We were extremely impressed with the depth of this enterprise-class service offering, which was validated by the breadth of adoption by their large and sophisticated customer base. eVisit has an early seat in this expanding market, and we are thrilled to be a part of a journey of helping to improve patient outcomes, while reducing cost."
title: EVisit是一家SaaS初创公司，帮助医院和医疗保健系统提供远程医疗服务，由TVC Capital牵头筹集1400万美元的首轮融资
title_en: eVisit, a SaaS startup that helps hospitals and health care systems provide telehealth services, raises $14M Series A led by TVC Capital

note:
几周前，我在WPTavern上添加了这条评论，在一篇文章中，WordPress创始人马特·穆伦韦格(Matt Mullenweg)澄清了他早先的言论，即Jamstack是绝大多数采用它的人的倒退。
因为我喜欢拥有自己的内容，所以我把它转载到我自己的博客里。
我认为马特残酷的诚实是值得欢迎的，因为大多数关于Jamstack的信息都对此赞不绝口。然而，它也来自开发人员使用这些现代新工具，评估自己的便利性和满意度。正如Matt指出的，这并不意味着它让终端用户更容易使用该软件，而这正是WordPress擅长的。
我实际上很喜欢Jamstack，但由于它非常复杂，即使要支持一些其他方面的基本功能，它也相当有限。
最明显的例子是评论，它应该放在构建社区的核心网站上。WordPress非常擅长支持网站中的评论。Jamstack在这方面太糟糕了。这么多年来，一直没有人能够解决Jamstack的注释问题，对我来说，这证明它本质上不适合支持这一特性。
Netlify Forms：没有层次结构，所以可以发布评论，但不能回复(除非在评论正文中添加一些元？这有多难看？)。
将评论存储在GitHub资源库中：将公关与评论合并需要很长时间。
而且，所有这些解决方案都非常复杂。我是否需要设置一个网络挂钩来触发新的构建，仅仅是为了添加评论？然后，是否可以在客户端的LocalStorage中缓存新注释，以备用户在新构建完成之前立即刷新页面？真的吗？
而且，它们没有提供杀手级的功能：向参与讨论的各方发送新评论的通知。社区就是这样建立起来的，网站就是这样成功的。速度是一个因素。但比速度更重要的是，支持社区的功能是动态的。这个网站可能看起来很花哨，但它很可能会变成一个鬼城。
(顺便说一句，作为练习，您可以研究哪些网站最初是wordpress，然后迁移到…，并检查它们当时有多少评论，而不是现在的Jamstack。这些数字很可能会大幅下降)。
另一种方法是不预呈现注释，而是在使用API获取注释后动态呈现它们。是的，这个解决方案是有效的，但是您仍然在后端有WordPress(或一些其他CM)来存储评论：p。
最后一种选择是使用第三方(如Disqus)为您处理此功能。然后，我将与第三方共享我的用户的数据，他们可能会使用它，谁知道如何使用它，以及为了谁的利益(最有可能的，不是我的用户的)。因为我关心隐私，所以这对我来说是一个很大的拒绝。
因此，我自己的博客(这是一个Jamstack站点)不支持评论！如果我想要博客帖子的反馈，我该怎么办？我在相应的tweet上添加了一个链接，要求在那里添加评论。我自己对这种妥协感到羞愧，但考虑到我的网站堆栈，我看不出我能如何解决这个问题。
不过，我仍然喜欢我的博客作为Jamstack，因为它速度快、免费，而且我使用VSCode在Markdown中创建所有博客帖子。但是我不能创建一个社区！所以，正如Matt所说，Jamstack可以处理一些事情。但肯定不是全部。也可能不是那些让你的网站变得成功的网站。
note_en:
A few weeks ago I added  this comment in WPTavern, on an article where WordPress founder's Matt Mullenweg clarifies his earlier remarks that the Jamstack is "a regression for the vast majority of the people adopting it".
 Since I like owing my own content, I reproduce it here in my own blog.
  I think Matt’s brutal honesty is welcome, because most information out there about the Jamstack praises it. However, it also comes from developers using these modern new tools, evaluating their own convenience and satisfaction. As Matt points out, that doesn’t mean it makes it easier for the end user to use the software, which is what WordPress is good at.
 I actually like the Jamstack, but because of how complex it is, it’s rather limiting, even to support some otherwise basic functionality.
 The definitive example is comments, which should be at the core websites building communities. WordPress is extremely good at supporting comments in the site. The Jamstack is sooooo bad at it. In all these many years, nobody has been able to solve comments for the Jamstack, which for me evidences that it is inherently unsuitable to support this feature.
  Netlify forms: no hierarchy, so can post a comment but not a response (unless adding some meta to the comment body? how ugly is that?)
 Storing comments in a GitHub repo: it takes a long time to merge the PR with the comment
 Also, all these solutions are overtly complicated. Do I need to set-up a webhook to trigger a new build just to add a comment? And then, maybe cache the new comment in the client’s LocalStorage for if the user refreshes the page immediately, before the new build is finished? Seriously?
 And then, they don’t provide the killer feature: to send notifications of the new comment to all parties involved in the discussion. That’s how communities get built, and websites become successful. Speed is a factor. But more important than speed, it is dynamic functionality to support communities. The website may look fancy, but it may well become a ghost town.
 (Btw, as an exercise, you can research which websites started as WordPress and then migrated to the Jamstack, and check how many comments they had then vs now… the numbers will, most likely, be waaaaaaay down)
 Another way is to not pre-render the comments, but render them dynamically after fetching it with an API. Yes, this solution works, but then you still have WordPress (or some other CMS) in the back-end to store the comments :P
 The final option is to use 3rd parties such as Disqus to handle this functionality for you. Then, I will be sharing my users’ data with the 3rd party, and they may use it who knows how, and for the benefit of who (most likely, not my users’). Since I care about privacy, that’s a big no for me.
 As a result, my own blog, which is a Jamstack site, doesn’t support comments! What do I do if I want feedback on a blog post? I add a link to a corresponding tweet, asking to add a comment there. I myself feel ashamed at this compromise, but given my site’s stack, I don’t see how I can solve it.
 I still like my blog as a Jamstack, though, because it’s fast, it’s free, and I create all the blog posts in Markdown using VSCode. But I can’t create a community! So, as Matt says, there are things the Jamstack can handle. But certainly not everything. And possibly, not the one(s) that enable your your website to become successful.
title: Jamstack在注释方面是如何失败的
title_en: How the Jamstack is failing at comments

note:
今天，美国司法部(DoJ)对谷歌提起反垄断诉讼，指控谷歌在搜索和搜索广告市场通过反竞争和排他性做法非法维持垄断。虽然我们仍在调查投诉，但我们的初步印象概述如下。
像数以百万计的日常互联网用户一样，我们都担心大型科技公司日益增长的实力如何阻碍创新，减少消费者的选择。我们相信，如果我们要建设一个更好的互联网，对这些问题的审查是健康的，也是至关重要的。我们还从第一手经验中知道，这些复杂问题不是一朝一夕就能解决的。Mozilla的起源与90年代针对微软的上一次重大反垄断案密切相关。
在这起新的诉讼中，司法部将谷歌与Mozilla的搜索协议作为谷歌垄断美国搜索引擎市场的一个例子。像Mozilla这样的小型独立公司通过创新、颠覆并在搜索等领域为用户提供业界领先的功能和服务而蓬勃发展。反垄断诉讼的最终结果不应该对像Mozilla这样最有能力推动竞争和保护网络消费者利益的组织造成附带损害。
在过去的20年里，Mozilla一直领导着浏览器市场内外的竞争、创新和消费者选择的斗争。我们在创造尊重消费者隐私和安全的创新产品和服务方面有着长期的记录，并成功地推动了市场的效仿。
执法行动对规模较小的创新者造成的意外伤害将损害整个体系，对消费者没有任何有意义的好处-而且也不是任何人都能修复大型科技公司的方式。相反，补救措施必须着眼于整个生态系统，并允许竞争和选择的蓬勃发展，以使消费者受益。
note_en:
Today the US Department of Justice (“DOJ”) filed an antitrust lawsuit against Google, alleging that it unlawfully maintains monopolies through anticompetitive and exclusionary practices in the search and search advertising markets. While we’re still examining the complaint our initial impressions are outlined below.
 Like millions of everyday internet users, we share concerns about how Big Tech’s growing power can deter innovation and reduce consumer choice. We believe that scrutiny of these issues is healthy, and critical if we’re going to  build a better internet. We also know from firsthand experience there is no overnight solution to these complex issues. Mozilla’s origins are closely tied to the last major antitrust case against Microsoft in the nineties.
 In this new lawsuit, the DOJ referenced Google’s search agreement with Mozilla as one example of Google’s monopolization of the search engine market in the United States. Small and independent companies such as Mozilla thrive by innovating, disrupting  and providing users with industry leading features and services in areas like search. The ultimate outcomes of an antitrust lawsuit should not cause collateral damage to the very organizations – like Mozilla – best positioned to drive competition and protect the interests of consumers on the web.
 For the past 20 years, Mozilla has been leading the fight for competition, innovation and consumer choice in the browser market and beyond. We have a long track record of creating innovative products and services that respect the privacy and security of consumers, and have successfully  pushed the market to follow suit.
 Unintended harm to smaller innovators from enforcement actions will be detrimental to the system as a whole, without any meaningful benefit to consumers — and is not how anyone will fix Big Tech. Instead, remedies must look at the ecosystem in its entirety, and allow the flourishing of competition and choice to benefit consumers.
title: Mozilla对美国与谷歌的反应
title_en: Mozilla Reaction to U.S. vs. Google

note:
为了减少在接触冠状病毒后被送回家隔离的学生数量，蒙大拿州最大的学区比林斯公立学校(Billings Public School)想出了一个让公共卫生专家摇头的想法：每小时在教室里重新洗四次学生。
该策略是基于对需要隔离的“密切接触”的定义-在距离感染者6英尺的范围内15分钟或更长时间。如果学生在这段时间内四处走动，这种想法是这样的，没有人会有“密切接触”，如果同学检测呈阳性，就不会要求他们呆在家里。
这个拥有1.65万名学生的学区负责人格雷格·厄普汉姆(Greg Upham)在接受采访时表示，追踪联系人已经成为该学区的巨大负担，管理人员在提出这一运动想法时，正在寻找减轻负担的方法。他说，这并不是为了“玩弄制度”，而是为了鼓励工作人员认识到15分钟的窗口。
在上周发给管理人员的一封电子邮件中，厄普汉姆鼓励员工“只要有可能，就通过移动、疏远和掩饰来扰乱15分钟的时间表。”
传染病专家说，每隔几分钟让学生四处走动实际上更有可能增加病毒的传播，因为这会使更多的人接触到受感染的学生。他们说，这也将使接触者追踪工作复杂化。
约翰·霍普金斯健康安全中心(John Hopkins Center For Health Security)的流行病学家詹妮弗·努佐(Jennifer Nuzzo)博士一直支持重新开放学校进行面对面教学，她说，“这不是一种基于证据的做法，也不是合理的科学政策。”
努佐说，15分钟6英尺长的定义是用来确定谁可能感染风险更高的准则，而不是关于何时可以或不可能感染的硬性规定。他补充说，一个人肯定可以在更短的时间内或从更远的地方感染，特别是在室内。
哈佛大学公共卫生学院免疫学和传染病系主任莎拉·福琼博士说，15分钟的定义是为了帮助接触追踪者有效和高效地识别风险最高的人，并对他们进行有针对性的干预。
比林斯西部高中(Billings West High School)校长凯利·霍恩比(Kelly Hornby)上周在给员工的一封电子邮件中写道，每隔几分钟让学生们四处走动，然后让他们回到原来的课桌上，将有助于驱散空气中含有冠状病毒的微滴，达到“被污染的风险大大降低”的程度。
“财富”博士不同意这一观点。她说：“传播Covid的颗粒悬浮在空气中，它们在空气中传播，气溶胶可以在空气中停留很长时间。”“因此，把空气吹起来或从你的位置四处走动并不能真正限制你的暴露或风险。”
note_en:
To reduce the number of students sent home to quarantine after exposure to the coronavirus, the Billings Public Schools, the largest school district in Montana, came up with an idea that has public health experts shaking their heads: Reshuffling students in the classroom four times an hour.
 The strategy is based on the definition of a “close contact” requiring quarantine — being within 6 feet of an infected person for 15 minutes or more. If the students are moved around within that time, the thinking goes, no one will have had “close contact” and be required to stay home if a classmate tests positive.
 Greg Upham, the superintendent of the 16,500-student school district, said in an interview that contact tracing had become a huge burden for the district, and administrators were looking for a way to ease the burden when they came up with the movement idea. It was not intended to “game the system,” he said, but rather to encourage the staff to be cognizant of the 15-minute window.
 In an email to administrators last week, Mr. Upham encouraged staff to “whenever possible, disrupt the 15-minute timeline through movement, distancing, and masking.”
 Infectious disease experts say that moving students around every few minutes is actually more likely to increase transmission of the virus, by exposing more people to an infected student. It will also complicate contact tracing efforts, they said.
 “That is not an evidence-based practice or sound scientific policy,” said Dr. Jennifer Nuzzo, an epidemiologist at the John Hopkins Center for Health Security who has been supportive of  reopening schools for in-person instruction.
 The 15-minute, 6-foot definition is a guideline for identifying who might be at greater risk of infection, not a hard-and-fast rule about when it can or cannot happen, Dr. Nuzzo said, adding that a person can certainly become infected in less time or from farther away, especially indoors.
 Dr. Sarah Fortune, chair of the department of immunology and infectious diseases at Harvard’s school of public health, said the 15-minute definition was meant to help contact tracers “effectively and efficiently identify people with the highest risk and target intervention to them.”
 Kelly Hornby, principal of Billings West High School, wrote in an email to his staff last week that moving students around every few minutes and then returning them to their original desks would help dissipate airborne droplets containing coronavirus, to the point “where the risk of being contaminated is greatly reduced.”
 Dr. Fortune disagreed with that idea. “The particles that transmit Covid, they hang out in the air, and they spread through the air, and the aerosols can hang out for a very long time,” she said. “So stirring that air up or moving around from your spot doesn’t really limit your exposure or risk.”
title: 为了避免隔离学生，学区每15分钟转移一次。
title_en: To avoid quarantining students, school district moves them every 15 minutes

该公司通过WarnerMedia Ride将其优质视频节目带给无聊的孩子和其他厌倦道路的乘客，WarnerMedia Ride是一项新的视频服务，适用于连接到无线网络的车载Wi-Fi热点的智能手机和平板电脑-免费提供。
WarnerMedia Ride中的内容包括来自Bleacher Report、飞镖、卡通网络、CNN、TBS、TNT等品牌的新闻和体育节目。这项服务将免费提供给27个汽车品牌的美国车主，他们拥有美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)的无限数据联网计划。乘客可以在苹果的App Store(需要iOS 11或更高版本)或Google Play(Android 9或更高版本)上下载WarnerMedia Ride应用程序。
2021年，美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)计划为联网汽车数据计划订户提供约1万小时视频节目的完整内容库，作为新的连接和内容捆绑包的一部分。
有什么意义？美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)希望利用WarnerMedia的内容作为与联网汽车客户签约的胡萝卜，而WarnerMedia则看到了接触新的俘虏受众的潜力。
美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)推出WarnerMedia Ride的旗舰合作伙伴是。目前，超过100万通用客户订购了AT&amp；T提供的支持Wi-Fi的数据服务。通用雪佛兰(Chevrolet)、别克(Buick)、通用汽车(GMC)和凯迪拉克(Cadillac)某些车型的车主都可以享受这项服务。
此外，WarnerMedia Ride还将面向AT&amp；T汽车品牌的无线用户，包括：阿库拉、阿尔法·罗密欧、奥迪、宝马、克莱斯勒、道奇、福特、本田、英菲尼迪、捷豹、吉普、路虎、雷克萨斯、林肯、马自达、梅赛德斯-奔驰、日产、保时捷、劳斯莱斯、RAM、斯巴鲁、丰田和沃尔沃。
两家公司表示，WarnerMedia Ride服务仅在车辆运行时供乘客使用。美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T；)推出车载流媒体视频服务，是在宣传其“可以等待”的活动，敦促顾客不要分心开车。
AT&amp；T的车载Wi-Fi用户已经可以访问WarnerMedia Audio，其中包括70多个播客和数百小时的音频内容，包括来自CNN和其他WarnerMedia新闻网络的现场音频联播。
通用汽车全球互联服务副总裁圣地亚哥·查莫罗(Santiago Chamorro)在一份声明中表示：“WarnerMedia播客、电影和电视节目库的加入--与AT&amp；T；的Wi-Fi连接相结合--只是我们为客户提升拥有体验的另一种方式。”
WarnerMedia Content Experience副总裁Barry Loudis补充说：“与通用汽车的这种关系意味着我们可以将车主与新的身临其境的体验和独家内容完全联系起来，为整个家庭创造有意义的联系。”
WarnerMedia Ride包括一个新的身份验证功能，一旦应用程序检测到车辆的Wi-Fi热点，用户就可以访问内容。
据通用汽车称，其目前100万AT&amp；数据用户主要是拥有SUV的家庭，如雪佛兰Tahoe和郊区、GMC育空和凯迪拉克凯雷德(Cadillac Escalade)。美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)和通用汽车(GM)自2014年以来一直在合作，通过高速无线连接将车辆连接起来。
note_en:
is bringing its premium video programming to bored kids and other road-weary passengers through WarnerMedia Ride, a new video service for smartphones and tablets connected to in-car Wi-Fi hotspots connected to  ’s wireless network — available at no extra cost.
 Content in WarnerMedia Ride includes news and sports programming from Bleacher Report, Boomerang, Cartoon Network, CNN, TBS, TNT and other brands. The service will be available for free to U.S. owners of 27 vehicle brands who have AT&T unlimited data connected-car plans. Passengers can download the WarnerMedia Ride app on Apple’s App Store (requires iOS 11 or later) or Google Play (Android 9 or later).
  In 2021, AT&T plans to offer the full   content library of around 10,000 hours of video programming as part of a new bundle of connectivity and content for connected-car data plan subscribers.
  What’s the point? AT&T wants to use the WarnerMedia content as a carrot to sign up connected-car customers, while WarnerMedia sees the potential to reach a new captive audience.
 AT&T’s flagship partner for the WarnerMedia Ride launch is  . Currently, more than 1 million GM customers subscribe to Wi-Fi-enabled data services provided by AT&T. The service is available to owners of certain models of GM’s Chevrolet, Buick, GMC and Cadillac vehicles.
 In addition, WarnerMedia Ride will be available to AT&T in-car wireless subscribers in car brands including: Acura, Alfa Romeo, Audi, BMW, Chrysler, Dodge, Ford, Honda, Infiniti, Jaguar, Jeep, Land Rover, Lexus, Lincoln, Mazda, Mercedes-Benz, Nissan, Porsche, Rolls Royce, RAM, Subaru, Toyota and Volvo.
 The WarnerMedia Ride service is intended for passenger use only when a vehicle is in operation, the companies said. In launching the in-car streaming video service, AT&T is promoting its “It Can Wait” campaign to urge customers to not drive distracted.
 AT&T’s in-car Wi-Fi customers already have access to WarnerMedia Audio, which comprises more than 70 podcasts and hundreds of hours of audio content including live audio simulcasts from CNN and other WarnerMedia news networks.
 “The addition of WarnerMedia’s library of podcasts, movies and television programming — combined with AT&T’s Wi-Fi connectivity — is just another way we’re enhancing the ownership experience for our customers,” said Santiago Chamorro, VP of GM Global Connected Services, in a statement.
 Added Barry Loudis, vice president, WarnerMedia Content Experiences, “This relationship with GM means we can fully connect car owners with new immersive experiences and exclusive content to create meaningful connections for the whole family.”
 WarnerMedia Ride includes a new authentication feature that lets users access content once the app detects a vehicle’s Wi-Fi hotspot.
 According to GM, its current base of 1 million AT&T data subscribers primarily are families with SUVs like the Chevrolet Tahoe and Suburban, GMC Yukon and Cadillac Escalade. AT&T and GM have worked together since 2014 on connecting vehicles with high-speed wireless connectivity.
title: WarnerMedia首次推出WarnerMedia Ride，这是一项免费视频服务，适用于通过AT&T的无限数据联网汽车计划连接到车载WiFi的智能手机和平板电脑
title_en: WarnerMedia debuts WarnerMedia RIDE, a free video service for smartphones and tablets connected to in-car WiFi via AT&T's unlimited data connected-car plans

note:
生存偏见指的是，当我们只根据那些活着讲述故事的人的经历来理解时，我们就会得到对现实的错误描述。看看我们是如何歪曲鲨鱼袭击的，突出了生存偏见在其他情况下是如何扭曲现实的。
当被问及最致命的鲨鱼对人类来说是什么时，大多数人会说大白鲨。电影“大白鲨”(Jaws)的持久影响力，再加上数十篇流行文化的引用和新闻报道，让人们在考虑到世界上最可怕的捕食者时，会把这种鲨鱼放在脑海中的首位。虽然大白鲨确实攻击人类(很少)，但它们也留下了很多幸存者。而且它们也不是特别针对人类。它们通常会把我们误认为海豹，海豹是它们的主要食物来源之一。
我们必须小心，不要让一个地区的大量幸存者蒙蔽了我们，使我们看不到其他地方少数幸存者的故事。最重要的是，我们需要问问自己，有哪些故事没有被讲述，因为周围没有人来讲述它们。如果我们想要准确地理解这个世界，死者的经历是必要的。
在我们深入研究一些有趣的统计数据之前，重要的是要了解大白鲨是鲨鱼类中具有许多共同特征的一员。大白鲨与虎鲨和牛鲨关系密切。它们都有相似的栖息地、生理和本能。它们也都很大，平均体型超过10英尺长。
老虎和牛鲨很少攻击人类，对于被这些巨大生物中的一种咬伤的人来说，它们之间没有太大的区别。佛罗里达博物馆的国际鲨鱼袭击文件解释说，“确定攻击鲨鱼的身份是非常困难的，因为受害者很少在互动的‘激烈’期间对袭击者进行充分的观察。牙齿残骸很少在伤口中发现，许多安魂鲨(大白鲨就是其中之一)的诊断特征即使是训练有素的专业人士也很难辨别。“。
在已知的袭击中，牛鲨的死亡率为21.5%，大白鲨为16%，虎鲨为26%。但就数量而言，大白鲨的袭击数量是其他两个物种的三倍。所以有三倍多的幸存者来讲述他们大白鲨袭击的故事。
当谈到我们对最危险鲨鱼的现实图景时，还有其他盲点。并不是所有的鲨鱼都有与这三种鲨鱼相同的行为，比如靠近海岸游泳，以及周围有足够的猎物，从而形成了对肥海豹的偏好，而不是骨瘦如柴的人类。远洋鲨鱼生活在开阔海域的水沙漠中，它们必须吃几乎所有它们能找到的东西。海洋中的白鳍鲨是一种远洋鲨鱼，对人类来说可能要危险得多--我们只是不经常接触它们。
只有15起记录在案的海洋白鳍豚袭击事件，其中3起是致命的。但由于大多数袭击发生在开阔的海洋中，发生在更孤立的情况下(例如，几个人在船上，而500个人在海滩游泳)，我们真的不知道海洋中的白色尖端有多危险。可能会有数百起未经记录的袭击事件，没有幸存者可以讲述这个故事。
一个著名的幸存者故事让我们得以一窥海洋白色尖端可能有多危险。1945年，一艘日本潜艇击落了印第安纳波利斯号航空母舰。由于多种原因，部分原因是印第安纳波利斯号正在执行一项绝密任务，部分原因是悲剧性的无能，四天内没有派出救援船。那些在沉船中幸存下来的人不得不试着在开阔的海洋中几乎没有装备的情况下生存，直到救援到来。水里全是鲨鱼。
在“印第安纳波利斯：美国海军史上最严重海难的真实故事”一书中，林恩·文森特(Lynn Vincent)和莎拉·弗拉迪奇(Sara Vladic)引用水手长的二等搭档尤金·摩根(Eugene Morgan)的话描述了他的部分经历：“一直以来，鲨鱼从未停止过。我们有一个货网，上面附着着聚苯乙烯泡沫塑料的东西来维持它的漂浮。船上大约有15名水手，突然，10条鲨鱼袭击了它，什么也没有留下。这种情况一直在继续。“。这些鲨鱼被认为是海洋中的白鳍鲨。尚不清楚有多少人死于鲨鱼袭击。许多人也因暴露、脱水、受伤和精疲力竭而死亡。在最初登上这艘船的1195名船员中，只有316人生还。这是美国海军历史上单艘舰艇造成的最大一次生命损失。
由于人类很少大量出现在开阔的海洋中，不仅这种鲨鱼袭击的事件不常见，而且幸存者的故事也更少了。印第安纳波利斯号航空母舰的故事是一个罕见的、残酷的案例，提供了一幅独特的图景。
我们对可能对我们造成最大伤害的鲨鱼的估计往往是由生存偏见形成的。我们根据那些活着讲述鲨鱼袭击故事的人的故事，绘制了一幅不准确的图片。我们不会问自己谁没有幸存下来，所以我们错过了我们需要的信息来构建一幅准确的现实图景。
重点不是要把我们的恐惧转移到海洋的白色尖端，事实上，它们是极度濒危的。我们对鲨鱼的恐惧似乎让我们对发生在它们身上的事情漠不关心，尽管它们是海洋生态系统的重要组成部分。我们对鲨鱼的威胁也比它们对我们的威胁大得多。我们每年都会杀死数以百万计的它们。我们也不应该把我们的恐惧转移到其他更致命的动物身上，这可能会导致我们对它们在生态系统中的作用同样漠不关心。
更重要的是，当你只考虑幸存者的故事时，要考虑你做决定的能力。例如，如果你试图减少鲨鱼袭击事件的发生，或者试图限制其严重性，如果你只关注幸存者的故事，你可能不会得到你想要的结果。你需要问问谁没有成功，并试着找出他们的故事。如果你试图实施只针对海滩附近的大白鲨的措施，你的措施可能对其他捕食性鲨鱼无效。如果你的结论是，因为鲨鱼似乎只在海滩附近袭击，所以游泳者在开阔的海洋里更好，那你就大错特错了。
生存偏见在我们的生活中随处可见，阻碍我们准确评估危险。把“危险的鲨鱼”换成“危险的城市”或“危险的度假胜地”，你就可以很容易地看到基于幸存者的经历，你的某个地点的图片可能会被歪曲。如果没有人活着讲故事，我们就不能害怕它。更多的幸存者可以让一些事情看起来更危险，而不是更不危险，因为故事的数量让它们更令人难忘。
如果更少的人在鲨鱼袭击中幸存下来，我们就不会有幸存者的故事影响我们对鲨鱼有多危险的看法。我们很可能会将一些海洋死亡归因于其他原因，比如溺水，因为我们不会想到鲨鱼应该对此负责。
理解生存偏见促使我们寻找那些失败者的故事。缺乏具有令人难忘的故事的看得见的幸存者可能意味着我们认为其他领域比它们更安全、更容易。
例如，一个经历过失败的人继续做其他事情的商业领域，似乎比一个失败的人羞于谈论它的领域风险更大。科技初创公司的失败有时感觉像是每天的新闻。然而，我们并不经常听说房地产经纪人在销售方面遇到困难，或者他的出价一直高于报价。我们也没有听到太多关于设计糟糕房屋的建筑师或没有完成项目的建筑公司的消息。
生存偏差促使我们将更多的风险与表现出更多公共失灵的行业联系起来。但是，没有分享的行业或企业的失败也同样重要。如果我们只关注幸存者的故事，我们可能会认为当一名房地产经纪人或建筑师比创办一家科技公司更安全。也许是这样，但我们不能仅仅基于广泛分享的失败故事来理解哪种职业选择是最好的赌注。
如果我们不把生存偏见考虑到我们的思维中，我们最终会得到一张经典的地图，而不是领土问题。幸存者的故事成了该地区糟糕的导航工具。
我们大多数人都知道，我们不应该基于J·K·罗琳和约翰·格里沙姆取得的成果而成为一名作家。但是，即使我们出去和其他作家交谈，或者了解他们的职业生涯，或者参加出版作家的写作研讨会，我们仍然只和幸存者交谈。
是的，得知斯蒂芬·金在职业生涯早期遭到如此多的拒绝，以至于堆积如山的拒绝足以让人大吃一惊，这是非常鼓舞人心的。但是，那些同样遭到拒绝，从未发表过任何作品的作家又该怎么办呢？我们不仅可以从他们那里学到很多关于出版业的知识，如果我们想要预测和理解作为一名作家所面临的挑战，我们还需要考虑他们的经历。
没有认识到生存偏见可能会导致错误的决策。我们没有看到大的图景，最终只针对一小部分现实进行了优化。我们不能完全克服生存偏见。我们能做的最多就是承认这一点，当赌注很高或结果很重要时，停下来寻找那些不成功的人的故事。他们有同样多的东西可以教给我们，如果不是更多的话。
下次你评估风险时，问问自己：我是不是太关注大白鲨了，而对海洋中的白鲨关注不够？
note_en:
Survivorship bias refers to the idea that we get a false representation of reality when we base our understanding only on the experiences of those who live to tell their story. Taking a look at how we misrepresent shark attacks highlights how survivorship bias distorts reality in other situations.
 When asked what the deadliest shark is to humans, most people will say the great white. The lasting influence of the movie Jaws, reinforced by dozens of pop culture references and news reports, keeps that species of shark at the top of the mind when one considers the world’s most fearsome predators. While it is true that great white sharks do attack humans (rarely), they also leave a lot of survivors. And they’re not after humans in particular. They usually just mistake us for seals, one of their key food sources.
 We must be careful to not let a volume of survivors in one area blind us to the stories of a small number of survivors elsewhere. Most importantly,  we need to ask ourselves what stories are not being told because no one is around to tell them. The experiences of the dead are necessary if we want an accurate understanding of the world.
  Before we drill down into some interesting statistics, it’s important to understand that great whites are one member of a class of sharks with many common characteristics. Great whites are closely related to tiger and bull sharks. They all have similar habitats, physiology, and instincts. They are also all large, with an average size over ten feet long.
 Tiger and bull sharks rarely attack humans, and to someone being bit by one of these huge creatures, there isn’t all that much difference between them. The Florida Museum’s International Shark Attack file explains that  “positive identification of attacking sharks is very difficult since victims rarely make adequate observations of the attacker during the ‘heat’ of the interaction. Tooth remains are seldom found in wounds and diagnostic characters for many requiem sharks [of which the great white is one] are difficult to discern even by trained professionals.”
 The fatality rate in known attacks is 21.5% for the bull shark, 16% for the great white, and 26% for the tiger shark. But in sheer volume, attacks attributed to great whites outnumber the other two species three to one. So there are three times as many survivors to tell the story of their great white attack.
  When it comes to our picture of reality of the most dangerous shark, there are other blind spots. Not all sharks have the same behaviors as those three, such as swimming close to shore and being around enough prey to develop a preference for fat seals versus bony humans. Pelagic sharks live in the water desert that is the open ocean and have to eat pretty much whatever they can find. The oceanic white tip is a pelagic shark that is probably far more dangerous to humans—we just don’t come into contact with them as often.
 There are only fifteen documented attacks by an oceanic white tip, with three of those being fatal. But since most attacks occur in the open ocean in more isolated situations (e.g., a couple of people on a boat versus five hundred people swimming at a beach), we really have no idea how dangerous oceanic white tips are. There could be hundreds of undocumented attacks that left behind no survivors to tell the tale.
 One famous survivor story gives us a glimpse of how dangerous oceanic white tips might be. In 1945, a Japanese submarine shot down the USS Indianapolis. For a multitude of reasons, partly due to the fact that the Indianapolis was on a top secret mission and partly due to tragic incompetence, a rescue ship was not sent for four days. Those who survived the ship’s sinking had to then try to survive in the open ocean with little gear until rescue arrived. The water was full of sharks.
 In   Indianapolis: The True Story of the Worst Sea Disaster in US Naval History and the Fifty-Year Fight to Exonerate an Innocent Man, Lynn Vincent and Sara Vladic quote Boatswain’s Mate Second Class Eugene Morgan as he described part of his experience: “All the time, the sharks never let up. We had a cargo net that had Styrofoam things attached to keep it afloat. There were about fifteen sailors on this, and suddenly, ten sharks hit it and there was nothing left. This went on and on.” These sharks are believed to have been oceanic white tips. It’s unknown how many men died from shark attacks. Many also perished due to exposure, dehydration, injury, and exhaustion. Of the 1,195 crewmen originally aboard the ship, only 316 survived. It represents the single biggest loss of life from a single ship in US naval history.
 Because humans are rarely in the open ocean in large numbers, not only are attacks by this shark less common, there are also fewer survivor stories. The story of the USS Indianapolis is a rare, brutal case that provides a unique picture.
  Our estimation of the shark that could do us the most harm is often formed by survivorship bias. We develop an inaccurate picture based on the stories of those who live to tell the tale of their shark attack. We don’t ask ourselves who didn’t survive, and so we miss out on the information we need to build an accurate picture of reality.
 The point is not to shift our fear to oceanic white tips, which are, in fact, critically endangered. Our fear of sharks seems to make us indifferent to what happens to them, even though they are an essential part of the ocean ecosystem. We are also much more of a danger to sharks than they are to us. We kill them by the millions every year. Neither should we shift our fear to other, more lethal animals, which will likely result in the same indifference to their role in the ecosystem.
 The point is rather to consider how well you make decisions when you only factor in the stories of the survivors. For instance, if you were to try to reduce instances of shark attacks or try to limit their severity, you will not likely get the results you are after if you only pay attention to the survivor stories. You need to ask who didn’t make it and try to figure out their stories as well. If you try to implement measures aimed only at great whites near beaches, your measures might not be effective against other predatory sharks. And if you conclude that swimmers are better off in the open ocean because sharks seem to only attack near beaches, you’d be completely wrong.
  Survivorship bias crops up all over our lives and impedes us from accurately assessing danger. Replace “dangerous sharks” with “dangerous cities” or “dangerous vacation spots” and you can easily see how your picture of a certain location might be skewed based on the experiences of survivors. We can’t be afraid of a tale if no one lives to tell it. More survivors can make something seem more dangerous rather than less dangerous because the volume of stories makes them more memorable.
 If fewer people survived shark attacks we wouldn’t have survivor stories influencing our perception about how dangerous sharks are. In all likelihood we would attribute some of the ocean deaths to other causes, like drowning, because it wouldn’t occur to us that sharks could be responsible.
 Understanding survivorship bias prompts us to look for the stories of those who weren’t successful.  A lack of visible survivors with memorable stories might mean we view other fields as far safer and easier than they are.
 For example, a field of business where people who experience failures go on to do other things might seem riskier than one where people who fail are too ashamed to talk about it. The failure of tech start-ups sometimes feels like daily news. We don’t often, however, hear about the real estate agent who has trouble making sales or who keeps getting outbid on offers. Nor do we hear much about architects who design terrible houses or construction companies who don’t complete projects.
 Survivorship bias prompts us to associate more risk with industries that exhibit more public failures. But the failures from industries or businesses that aren’t shared are equally important. If we focus only on the survivor stories, we might think that being a real estate agent or an architect is safer than starting a technology company. It might be, but we can’t only base our understanding on which career option is the best bet on the widely shared stories of failure.
 If we don’t factor survivorship bias into our thinking we end up in a classic  map is not the territory problem. The survivor stories become a poor navigational tool for the terrain.
 Most of us know that we shouldn’t become a writer based on the results achieved by J.K Rowling and John Grisham. But even if we go out and talk to other writers, or learn about their careers, or attend writing seminars given by published authors, we are still only talking to the survivors.
 Yes, it’s super inspiring to know Stephen King got so many rejections early in his career that the stack of them was enough to pull a nail out of the wall. But what about the writers who got just as many rejections and never published anything? Not only can we learn a lot from them about the publishing industry, we need to consider their experiences if we want to anticipate and understand the challenges involved in being a writer.
  Not recognizing survivorship bias can lead to faulty decision making. We don’t see the big picture and end up optimizing for a small slice of reality. We can’t completely overcome survivorship bias. The best we can do is acknowledge it, and when the stakes are high or the result important, stop and look for the stories of those who were unsuccessful. They have just as much, if not more, to teach us.
 The next time you’re assessing risk, ask yourself: am I paying too much attention to the great white sharks and not enough to the oceanic white tips?
title: 关于生存偏见，鲨鱼能教给我们什么？
title_en: What sharks can teach us about survivorship bias

note:
过去在学校里普遍教授的大流行与天花或鼠疫耶尔森氏菌有关。鼠疫耶尔森氏菌是一种引起鼠疫的细菌，也被称为黑鼠疫。黑鼠疫的死亡人数约为2500万人，在接下来的300年里也发生了几次死灰复燃，导致了数百万人的伤亡。鼠疫耶尔森氏菌也被认为是483年至565年间查士丁尼瘟疫的罪魁祸首，查士丁尼瘟疫是有记录以来最早的瘟疫之一。
天花导致的死亡人数比历史上任何一次大流行都要多。最广为人知的天花疫情发生在1519年至1520年之间的墨西哥。据说它已经夺走了大约80%的当地人口。总伤亡人数估计在500万至800万之间。然而，在1877年至1977年间，天花夺去了5亿多人的生命。
西班牙流感是历史上另一种最严重的流行病，它的死亡率比天花快得多：1918年至1919年间，它导致5000多万人死亡。
说新冠肺炎是我们一生中最重大的流行病是值得商榷的，但它肯定是传播最快的。然而，艾滋病毒/艾滋病造成的死亡人数要多得多，自1981年以来已超过3200万人。新冠肺炎在不到一年的时间里杀死了108万多人。这些疾病在很多方面都不同，但由于新冠肺炎的空气传播性质，它导致了许多国家的全国性隔离。它已经影响了全球人民的生活方式。
纵观全球大流行历史，随着时间的推移，人类开发出了预防和抗击大流行的新方法。在黑死病期间，水手们会在船上呆上40天。这是第一个有记录的隔离证据，“隔离”一词源自意大利语中的“40”，至今仍保留在那个时代，尽管它不一定是40天长。我们还开发了疫苗，第一种是用来对抗天花的。爱德华·詹纳博士发现感染牛痘(一种温和得多的疾病)的患者似乎对天花免疫，所以他开始让健康人接触牛痘。今天，疫苗是预防疾病的基本方法。事实上，广泛接种天花疫苗导致了这种疾病的根除。
note_en:
Past pandemics that are commonly taught about in schools were associated with either smallpox or Yersinia pestis. Yersinia pestis is a bacterium that was responsible for the Bubonic Plague, also known as the Black Plague. The Black Plague death toll was around 25 million people, and there were also several resurgences over the course of the following 300 years, leading to millions more casualties. Yersinia pestis is also thought to have been responsible for the Plague of Justinian, one of the earliest recorded plagues, between the years of 483 and 565.
 Smallpox has killed more people than any of the other pandemics in history. The best-known smallpox epidemic was between 1519 and 1520 in Mexico. It’s said to have claimed roughly 80% of the native population. The total number of casualties is estimated between 5 and 8 million. However, between 1877 and 1977, smallpox killed more than 500 million people.
 The Spanish Flu, another of the worst pandemics in history, had a much faster death rate than smallpox: It killed more than 50 million people between 1918 and 1919.
  To say that COVID-19 is the most significant pandemic of our lifetime is up for debate, but it’s certainly the fastest-spreading. However,  HIV/AIDS has been responsible for far more deaths, more than 32 million since 1981. COVID-19 has killed more than 1,080,000 people in less than a year. The diseases are different in many ways, but due to the airborne nature of COVID-19, it has led to nationwide quarantines in many countries. It has affected the way of life of people all across the globe.
  Throughout global pandemic history, mankind has developed new ways to prevent and fight pandemics over time. During the Black Plague, sailors would remain on their ships for 40 days. This is the first recorded evidence of quarantines, and the word “quarantine,” derived from the Italian word for 40, remains from that era, even though it’s not necessarily 40 days long anymore. We’ve also developed vaccines, with the first one invented to combat smallpox. Dr. Edward Jenner discovered that patients infected with cowpox, a much milder illness, seemed immune to smallpox, so he began exposing healthy people to cowpox. Today, vaccines are an essential method of disease prevention. In fact, widespread vaccination against smallpox led to the eradication of this illness.
title: 历史上最致命的流行病

note:
华盛顿(CNN)特朗普政府各部门和机构的高级官员告诉CNN，他们对白宫的压力感到震惊，白宫要求将国防部的中频租赁合同授予Rivada Networks，这实际上是一份未经投标的合同--这是蓬勃发展和有利可图的5G市场的优质房地产。Rivada Networks是一家知名共和党人和唐纳德·特朗普总统的支持者投资的公司。
知情人士告诉CNN，9月份，白宫幕僚长马克·梅多斯(Mark Meadows)在特朗普的要求下领导了一场施压运动，要求通过使用权力来加快里瓦达征求建议书(RFP)的进程，这将阻止竞争性的竞标过程，并由白宫幕僚长马克·梅多斯(Mark Meadows)领导。为了推动他的案件，梅多斯有时会让一个被电信业消息人士确认为美军高级财务管理官员的人作为他的代理人。
消息人士告诉CNN，特朗普受到福克斯新闻评论员、资深共和党策略师卡尔·罗夫(Karl Rove)的鼓励，帮助里瓦达。卡尔·罗夫是里瓦达的游说者和投资者。
数不清的数十亿美元岌岌可危。8月份，政府拍卖了70兆赫的频谱，成交价超过45亿美元。Rivada的出价将是350兆赫的频谱--是这个数字的五倍。
罗夫向CNN否认里瓦达正在寻求RFP或任何非竞争性程序。他告诉CNN，如果我们得到一份没有投标的合同，我们会拒绝。这项技术应该独立存在。
尽管否认，但知情人士告诉CNN，白宫无疑正在向五角大楼施压，要求批准用一名高级政府官员的话说，很可能是历史上最大规模的经济权力移交给单一实体，而且这样做没有全面审查对国家安全的影响，也没有经过竞争性招标过程。
备受尊敬的华尔街电信业分析师克雷格·莫菲特(Craig Moffett)在10月7日的一篇研究论文中总结道：整个故事往好里说带有任人唯亲的味道，往坏里说则散发着沼泽的恶臭。
“光谱”非常有价值，罗夫告诉CNN。上帝不再创造了。可用于承载无线通信的频谱是有限的。
众议院前共和党议长、著名的特朗普支持者纽特·金里奇(Newt Gingrich)也一直在倡导五角大楼与商业用户共享5G网络，这样做的方式给内部人士留下的印象是，他曾为里瓦达游说。金里奇告诉CNN，他从来没有专门为里瓦达辩护，而且所有这些都是作为一名公民无偿进行的。
当被直接问及对白宫向官员施压要求里瓦达提供RFP的警告有何评论时，一名白宫官员告诉CNN，白宫在5G频谱方面并没有偏袒任何一家公司，并坚称对话仍在进行中，但仍处于决策前。无论如何，无论最终做出什么决定，都将是纳税人获得最佳成本/收益的结果，而不是出于任何政治原因。
Rivada的一位发言人否认该公司正在追求非竞争性的RFP。里瓦达发言人布莱恩·卡尼告诉CNN，绝对不是。我们一直主张竞争的过程。
特朗普的首席经济顾问拉里·库德洛(Larry Kudlow)一直反对中频频谱的自由市场竞争，中频频谱被视为5G的理想选择，因为它可以携带大量数据，同时还可以传输很远的距离。多年来，特朗普似乎同意库德洛的观点。然而，消息人士告诉CNN，随着选举时间的到来，情况发生了变化，总统开始敦促梅多斯帮助里瓦达。
消息人士猜测，特朗普可能一直在试图讨好罗夫，罗夫从来都不是Maga团队中可靠的成员，但在共和党政治中仍然是一股强大的筹款力量和策略师。
一种更温和的解读可能是，总统和梅多斯专注于让5G网络在全美同样快速、安全地传播，并对五角大楼和联邦通信委员会(Federal Communications Commission)的时间表和官僚作风感到失望。
消息人士告诉CNN，五角大楼领导人抵制这一举措，因为他们担心在没有彻底审查这可能对军事准备产生的影响的情况下仓促行事。五角大楼的律师告诉白宫，他们的部门无权以出租或出售频谱为目的发布RFP，他们认为，以白宫正在推动的方式这样做将完全背离正常的规章制度。
消息人士称，即使五角大楼有权这么做，并且对以非竞争性方式这样做并不感到不安，五角大楼官员也不确定里瓦达是否有能力将频谱出租用于商业用途，同时也保留了美国军方使用频谱进行军事演习和国防的能力。
里瓦达官员公开表示，他们有能力做到这两点。但在这一过程中，许多关键参与者并没有分享这种信心。五角大楼的一名官员告诉CNN，虽然Rivada相信它拥有共享频谱的最佳解决方案，但五角大楼尚未对这一断言进行审查，因此现在要求该公司提供RFP还为时过早。这名官员表示，如果获得RFP，那将是一座绝对的金矿。
FCC官员也对白宫的努力感到震惊，一方面是因为白宫试图利用其他机构而不是他们的独立委员会来做到这一点，另一方面是因为白宫试图在不尊重正常的独立竞标过程的情况下做到这一点。
国会山的民主党人和共和党人都很担心。众议院能源和商务委员会主席、新泽西州民主党众议员小弗兰克·帕隆(Frank Pallone Jr.)和相关小组委员会主席、宾夕法尼亚州民主党众议员迈克·多伊尔(Mike Doyle)最近要求提供更多信息，说明白宫是否已指示国防部立即进行提案请求，以推进全国5G网络的建设。(注：美国众议院能源和商务委员会主席小弗兰克·帕隆Jr.)和相关小组委员会主席、宾夕法尼亚州民主党众议员迈克·多伊尔(Mike Doyle)最近要求提供更多信息，说明白宫是否已指示国防部立即进行提案请求，以推进全国5G网络的建设。
据媒体报道，几名与特朗普总统或他的工作人员关系密切的政治工作人员或游说者……正在推动RFI设想的频谱政策的巨变。帕隆和多伊尔写道，这些报告还表明，这些共和党特工正在为一家名为Rivada，Inc.的特定公司工作，该公司长期以来一直支持Rivada使用其共享技术构建和运营一个全国性的网络。
发出危险信号的不仅仅是民主党人。9月底，南达科他州共和党参议员约翰·图恩(John Thune)和其他18名共和党参议员一起抗议任何可能的举措，这些举措不会依赖私营行业和市场力量来培育基于设施的多个5G网络，而是推动5G国有化，并试验未经测试的5G部署模式。共和党议员没有提到里瓦达的名字。
Rivada Networks是一家私人持股的通信公司，过去曾赢得过政府合同。它的投资者之一是著名的科技风险投资家、特朗普的支持者彼得·蒂尔(Peter Thiel)。Thiel的发言人没有回复记者的置评请求。
除了里瓦达的投资者和游说者的热情之外，许多政府官员对为什么里瓦达甚至被考虑签订这份利润丰厚的合同感到困惑。
在2016年和2017年，该公司寻求一项全国性的公共安全合同，第一响应网络管理局(First Responder Network Authority)，输给了美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T；)。(AT&amp；T是CNN的母公司，但与这篇报道有关联的人没有以任何方式与AT&T的任何人就这篇报道进行沟通。)。Rivada对这一决定提出上诉，联邦法院驳回了这一决定。
罗夫说，我们被拒绝的理由是我们没有向急救人员收取足够的费用。
法院不会这么说。在2017年的裁决中，法官伊莱恩·卡普兰(Elaine Kaplan)表示，由于里瓦达的财务状况和征集过程中的限制，银行援引这些条件的可能性高得令人无法接受，导致里瓦达甚至没有财政能力开始履行合同。
法院还指出，FirstNet评估称，Rivada(D)几乎没有能力、人员或财力；本身在……建设和运营全国无线网络方面没有实质性经验，在监督全国公共安全宽带网络规模和范围的项目方面缺乏任何经验。
我们认为FirstNet的评估委员会犯了错误，因为它认为Rivada Networks是一个独立的实体，正在竞购FirstNet，卡尼告诉CNN，并指出Rivada正在与几家大型通信公司组成的财团合作。但Rivada Networks没有竞购FirstNet。Rivada Mercury是由Rivada、Harris、Fujitsu和Black&amp；Veatch(IIRC)共同拥有的财团，竞购FirstNet。
据消息人士透露，今年早些时候，梅多斯要求五角大楼领导层研究如何才能腾出部分5G频谱供FCC拍卖，首席信息官达娜·迪希(Dana Deasy)被指派执行这项任务。
这项任务通常需要大约15个月的时间，但被快速跟踪到只需要15周。今年8月，白宫和五角大楼宣布计划释放100兆赫频段供拍卖。9月份，FCC提出了一项这样做的计划，预定拍卖结束日期为2021年12月。
观察家指出，这一过程正在按书进行，尽管速度非常快。另外，五角大楼在9月份发布了一份请求，要求提供关于应该如何处理Rivada关注的一个频段的信息。RFI回复的截止日期是周一。
消息人士告诉CNN，除此之外，不太透明的是白宫推动迫使五角大楼将这个中频频谱的控制权移交给Rivada。
当被问及该公司游说过谁时，罗夫和卡尼都不愿提供任何名字。卡尼告诉CNN，我们已经和政府中的人谈到了我们支持的内容。
note_en:
Washington (CNN)Senior officials throughout various departments and agencies of the Trump administration tell CNN they are alarmed at White House pressure to grant what would essentially be a no-bid contract to lease the Department of Defense's mid-band spectrum -- premium real estate for the booming and lucrative 5G market -- to Rivada Networks, a company in which prominent Republicans and supporters of President Donald Trump have investments.
 The pressure campaign to fast track Rivada's "Request for Proposal" (RFP) by using authorities that would preclude a competitive bidding process intensified in September, and has been led by White House chief of staff Mark Meadows, who was acting at Trump's behest, sources with knowledge tell CNN. To push his case, Meadows has sometimes used as his proxy an individual identified by sources in the telecommunications industry as a top financial management official in the US Army.
 Sources tell CNN that Trump was encouraged to help Rivada by Fox News commentator and veteran GOP strategist Karl Rove, a lobbyist for, and investor in, Rivada.
   Untold billions are at stake. A government auction of 70 megahertz of spectrum in August went for more than $4.5 billion. The Rivada bid would be for 350 megahertz of spectrum -- five times that amount.
 Rove denied to CNN that Rivada is seeking an RFP or any non-competitive process. "If we were offered a no-bid contract we would turn it down," he told CNN. "The technology should stand on its own."
  Denials notwithstanding, informed sources tell CNN that the White House is unquestionably pressuring the Pentagon to approve what would likely be, in the words of one senior administration official, "the biggest handoff of economic power to a single entity in history," and to do so without full examination of the impact on national security and without a competitive bidding process.
 Craig Moffett, a highly regarded Wall Street analyst of the telecommunications sector, concluded in a October 7 research paper: "The whole story smacks of cronyism at best and reeks of 'the swamp' at worst."
 "Spectrum is intensely valuable," Rove told CNN. "God is not inventing any more. There is a finite amount of spectrum available to carry wireless communications."
 Newt Gingrich, the former Republican speaker of the House and noted Trump supporter, has also been  advocating for the Pentagon to share its 5G network with commercial users, and doing so in a way that has left insiders under the impression that he lobbied for Rivada. Gingrich tells CNN he "never advocated for Rivada" specifically and "did all of it pro bono as a citizen."
 When asked directly for a comment about alarm that the White House was pressuring officials to offer Rivada the RFP, a White House official told CNN that "the White House has not taken a position in favor of any company over another with regard to 5G spectrum" and insisted that "conversations are ongoing but it is still pre-decisional. Regardless, whatever decision is ultimately made will be a result of best cost/benefit for the taxpayer, not any political reasons."
  A spokesman for Rivada denies that the company is pursuing a non-competitive RFP. "Absolutely not," Rivada spokesman Brian Carney told CNN. "We have always advocated for a competitive process."
 Trump's top economic adviser, Larry Kudlow, has been arguing against anything but free market competition for the mid-band spectrum, which is seen as ideal for 5G because it can carry loads of data while also traveling significant distances. For years, Trump seemingly agreed with Kudlow. Yet something changed come election time, sources tell CNN, and the President began pushing Meadows to help Rivada.
 Informed sources speculate that Trump may have been trying to curry favor with Rove, who has never been a reliable member of the MAGA team but remains a powerful fundraising force and strategist in GOP politics.
 A more benign interpretation might be that the President and Meadows are focused on having 5G networks spread as quickly and safely across the US and have been frustrated with Pentagon and Federal Communications Commission timelines and bureaucracy.
  Pentagon leaders are resisting the move, sources tell CNN, since they are concerned that this is being rushed without thoroughly vetting the impact it could have on military readiness. Pentagon lawyers have told the White House that their department has no authority to issue RFPs for the purpose of leasing or selling off its spectrum, and they think that to do so in the manner the White House is pushing would be a complete deviation from normal rules and regulations.
 Even if the Pentagon had the authority to do this, and didn't have qualms about doing it in a non-competitive way, sources say that Pentagon officials are unsure that Rivada has the ability to lease the spectrum for commercial use while also preserving the US military's ability to use the spectrum for military exercises and the defense of the country.
 Rivada officials are publicly confident about their ability to do both. But that confidence is not shared by many key players in this process. One Pentagon official told CNN that while Rivada believes it has the best solution for sharing the spectrum, the Pentagon hasn't vetted that assertion, so it would be premature to ask the company for an RFP, which would be "an absolute gold mine" if awarded, the official said.
 FCC officials have also been stunned at the White House's push, both at its attempt to do this using other agencies instead of their independent commission, and the attempt to do so without respecting the normal independent bidding process.
  Both Democrats and Republicans on Capitol Hill are concerned. House Energy and Commerce Committee Chairman Rep. Frank Pallone Jr., Democrat of New Jersey, and the chair of the relevant subcommittee, Rep. Mike Doyle, Democrat of Pennsylvania,  recently requested more information about whether "the White House has instructed DoD to proceed immediately to a Request for Proposal ('RFP') in order to move forward toward a national 5G network."
 "According to press accounts, several political operatives or lobbyists with close ties to President Trump or his staff...are pushing for the seismic shift in spectrum policy contemplated by the RFI. These reports also suggest these Republican operatives are working for the benefit of a specific company, Rivada, Inc., which has long championed a national network that Rivada would construct and operate using its sharing technology," Pallone and Doyle wrote.
 It's not just Democrats who have raised red flags. At the end of September, Sen. John Thune, Republican of South Dakota, along with 18 other GOP senators,  protested any potential move which would not "rely on private industry and market forces to foster multiple, facilities-based 5G networks" but rather push "nationalizing 5G and experimenting with untested models for 5G deployment." The GOP lawmakers did not mention Rivada by name.
  Rivada Networks is a privately-held communications company that has won government contracts in the past. Among its investors is Peter Thiel, the noted tech venture capitalist billionaire and Trump supporter. Spokespeople for Thiel did not respond to a request for comment.
 Beyond the enthusiasm of Rivada's investors and lobbyists, many administration officials are confused as to why Rivada is even being considered for the lucrative contract.
 In 2016 and 2017, the company pursued a nationwide public safety contract, First Responder Network Authority, losing out to AT&T. (AT&T is the parent company of CNN, but no one affiliated with this story has communicated with anyone at AT&T in any way about this story.) Rivada appealed that decision, which a federal court rejected.
 "The grounds on which we were turned down was that we did not charge first responders enough," Rove said.
 That's not how the court would put it. In her 2017  ruling, Judge Elaine Kaplan said that due to "Rivada's financial situation and the restrictions in the solicitation, there was an unacceptably high likelihood that the banks might invoke those conditions, leaving Rivada without the financial capacity to even begin performance on the contract."
 The court also noted that FirstNet had assessed that Rivada "possesse(d) few capabilities, staff, or financial resources"; did "not itself have material experience in...building and operating a nationwide wireless network"; and lacked any experience in "supervising a project of the size and scope" of the nationwide public safety broadband network.
 "We think that the evaluation committee in FirstNet erred because it judged Rivada Networks as a standalone entity bidding for FirstNet," Carney told CNN, noting that Rivada was working with a consortium of several large communications companies. But Rivada Networks didn't bid on FirstNet. Rivada Mercury, a consortium jointly owned by Rivada, Harris, Fujitsu and Black & Veatch (IIRC) bid on FirstNet."
  Earlier this year, Meadows asked Pentagon leadership to examine what could be done to free up some of this 5G spectrum for auction by the FCC, and chief information officer Dana Deasy was assigned to the task, according to sources.
 This task, which would normally take around 15 months, was fast-tracked to take only 15 weeks. In August, the White House and Pentagon announced plans to free up for auction 100 megahertz band of spectrum. In September the FCC proposed a plan for doing so, with a scheduled auction end-date of December 2021.
 This process is being done by the book, observers note, albeit extremely quickly. Separately, in September the Pentagon issued a Request for Information about what should be done with a band of spectrum that Rivada has its eyes on. The due date for the RFI responses was Monday.
 Beyond that, and less transparently, is the push by the White House to force the Pentagon to hand over control of this mid-band spectrum to Rivada, sources tell CNN.
    Neither Rove nor Carney would offer any names when asked who the company had lobbied. "We have spoken to people in the administration about what we support," Carney told CNN.
title: 白宫正试图加快国防部中频5G频谱的非投标合同
title_en: White House is trying to fast-track no-bid contract for DoD mid-band 5G spectrum

note:
彼得·乔伯斯(Peter Jobes)是一名科技和商业作家，曾与PA以及乐购(Tesco)和RAC等客户合作过。索尔维德的首席营销官。
社交媒体是一个随着时间的推移呈指数级增长的数字空间。尽管这是21世纪的发展，但它对世界-以及商业-的影响是震撼性的。
虽然企业一直忙于利用社交媒体作为宣传产品和服务的手段，但许多用户仍然选择使用平台来简单地消费他们关注的人的内容。
在社交平台上关注公众人物是一种巨大的趋势，品牌们广泛利用这一趋势寻找创新的方式来接触目标受众。这使得影响者在数百万人的生活中扮演着重要角色。
越来越多的人选择采用他们追随的社交媒体影响力者使用的生活方式和产品，而不是更深入地了解幕后发生的事情。当然，受影响者行为影响最大的用户是年轻人，他们可能更容易受到影响，更容易说服。
如上图所示，Instagram上活跃着大量有影响力的人。由于拥有如此多的用户和大量的追随者，平台可能很难监管用户看到的内容。
这意味着，年轻或更易受影响的受众可能不仅会接触到不道德的营销活动，还会接触到危险的活动、趋势或美容时尚，这些活动、趋势或美容时尚正被他们关注的账户纵容。
考虑到这一点，我们有必要问这样一个问题：影响者是否会对儿童造成损害？下面是对有影响力的人在网上拥有的权力的更深层次的了解：
虽然上面的图表可能看起来像是营销人员的梦想，但看到年轻人对Facebook、Twitter、YouTube和Instagram等社交媒体网站上有影响力的人的诚意有多么信任，这可能会让父母感到有些震惊。
当谈到社交媒体时，看似真实的用户正在积极影响儿童和年轻人的购买习惯。Instagram是一个典型的平台，在这个平台上，有影响力的用户可以用能够进行数字增强和过滤的完美图片来描绘自己的生活。
有影响力的人在Instagram上发布的照片是建立高追随者的关键，因此更多渴望获得更多追随者的用户不断寻求展示自己，以此作为完美的榜样。
这些精心策划的对有影响力的人的漫画正是孩子们最喜欢的，因为它使他们似乎可以达到无法达到的完美水平。
对有影响力的人的生活的洞察一直鼓舞着世界各地无数的儿童，而迷失对完美的追求可能会对年轻人的心理健康产生不利影响。
在英国，最近有消息称，近五分之一的儿童希望长大后自己也能成为社交媒体的影响力人物。
儿童，尤其是青少年，当他们与同龄人比较时，可以发展出一种认同感。当他们开始在网上与社交网络上过滤后的图片进行类似的比较时，这一自然过程可能会被扭曲。
根据最近发表在《儿科学》上的一项研究，孩子们如果看到那些拿着不健康零食的有影响力的人的照片，就更有可能因此食用不健康的食物。
有影响力的人文化最大的问题之一来自于对有影响力的人的看法有多有抱负。孩子们在网上有影响力的人的名气和品牌从属关系中找到了动力，许多人积极尝试在网上建立自己的个人资料，以期达到类似的受欢迎程度。
在如此巨大的舞台上尝试并有可能失败，结果可能会对孩子的自尊造成极坏的影响。
今天有很多孩子在网上积极参与有影响力的行为。他们不断地寻求创建和发布内容，以便在在线社交平台上积累更多的赞和分享。这直接影响到今天青少年和年轻人的自尊，他们现在可以通过评论和网上帖子的病毒式程度等可量化因素来衡量自己的成功。
这可能会带来有害的后果，因为他们试图在争取影响力水平的过程中实现生活的肤浅。
除了在易受影响的年轻人眼中失去影响力，以及他们可能产生的可怕心理影响之外，有影响力的用户在网上发布的内容类型也出现了一个大问题。
有影响力的人在他们制作的内容类型上很大程度上是不受监管的。过去，当年轻人发现电视名人和艺术家具有吸引力时，他们只会深入了解自己在媒体和各种出版物中的行为，这些媒体和出版物通常受到管理机构的良好监管，以避免他们报道的行为被纵容。
今天，有影响力的人有了与年轻人沟通的直接途径-这意味着他们可以直接向他们宣讲，而不需要知道他们造成的损害，也不需要手头的监管机构来阻止他们的内容播出。
因此，在向儿童推广赌博、进行露骨的嬉戏、纵容饮食失调以及鼓励极端美容治疗等方面，有影响力的人遇到了大量的争议。
最近有一个引人注目的例子，说明了有影响力的社交媒体用户对易受影响的观众的危险，一名TikTok用户记录下了自己用棉花花蕾在牙齿上摩擦过氧化氢的过程，以便让牙齿看起来更白。
在一段点击量超过1500万次的视频中，这位用户甚至在拍摄期间说：“如果你是牙医，不要告诉我这是错的。”随后，观看视频的用户选择自己进行危险的DIY治疗，并发布结果。
这段视频引起了忧心忡忡的牙医们的直言不讳。克里斯·斯特兰伯格在接受Health.com采访时说：“长时间使用这些高浓度的过氧化氢漂白，特别是连续使用多天，可能会导致牙龈高度刺激和牙齿敏感。”
如果在牙龈已经受到刺激的情况下进行更多的漂白，牙龈刺激会变得严重。牙齿敏感通常是暂时的(24小时左右)，但如果长期使用，显著的漂白可以永久性地增加牙齿的敏感度。“。
社交媒体用户倡导极端和非法的牙齿美白措施，而不是使用获得批准的产品，这一事实表明，年轻人渴望复制Instagram和其他网络上流行的完美水平。
随着TikTok最近的崛起，加上已经在蓬勃发展的网络名单，公平地说，社交媒体和沉浸在网上的有影响力的人不会很快消失。
虽然从这篇文章来看，这似乎不是一件好事，但重要的是要指出，许多有影响力的人为年轻人创造了安全的空间，以与志同道合的追随者建立社区感和友谊。
如果你担心有影响力的人可能会在联盟营销和鼓励不健康行为方面对你的孩子产生负面影响，那么与你的孩子开始对话是值得的。
通过问他们一些简单的问题，比如当有影响力的人推销产品时到底发生了什么，它可以帮助创造强大的影响力。
问问你的孩子，有影响力的人试图通过为他们展示一款产品来实现什么目标-这是为了让观众发笑吗？
询问有影响力的人是否提供了与产品配套的所有必要信息，并询问广告给您的孩子带来了什么感觉。
对有害的美容小贴士和纵容不健康行为也应该采取同样的方法。由于在线社交生态系统庞大且不断扩大，很难维持任何形式的监管，但通过帮助年轻人更多地意识到他们所看到的内容，有可能让社交网络上的生活对未来来说更加安全。
Discliamer：本文中的所有观点都属于作者，可能不反映整个Hacker Noon团队的观点。
note_en:
Peter Jobes is a tech and business writer having worked with PA and clients like Tesco, and RAC. CMO at Solvid.
 Social media is a digital space that has been expanding exponentially over time. Despite being a 21st Century development, its impact on the world - and business - has been seismic.
  While businesses have been busy utilizing social media as a means of advertising products and services, many users still choose to use platforms to simply consume the content of the people they follow.
  Following public figures on social platforms is a huge trend that’s widely leveraged by brands looking for innovative ways to reach their target audience. This has given rise to the role of the influencer in the lives of millions of individuals.
  Increasing numbers of people are choosing to adopt the lifestyle and products used by the social media influencers that they follow without looking deeper into what’s happening behind the scenes. Naturally, the users who are set to be most affected by the actions of influencers are young people who are likely to be more impressionable and easier to persuade.
     As the chart above shows, there’s a high volume of influencers active on Instagram. With such a large amount of users with high numbers of followers, it can be extremely difficult for platforms to regulate the content that users see.
  This means that young or more impressionable audiences may be exposed to not only amoral marketing campaigns but also dangerous activities, trends or beauty fads that are being condoned by the accounts that they follow.
  With this in mind, it’s worth asking the question, are influencers damaging for children? Here’s a deeper look into the power that influencers hold online:
      While the chart above may seem like a marketer’s dream, it may come across as somewhat alarming for parents to see how much young people believe in the sincerity of influencers on social media sites like Facebook, Twitter, YouTube and Instagram.
  When it comes to social media, seemingly real users are actively influencing children and young people in their purchasing habits. Instagram is a prime example of a platform where influential users can portray their life as perfect with flawless images that are capable of being digitally enhanced and filtered.
  The images posted to Instagram by influencers is key in building a high following, and so more users craving higher volumes of followers continually look to showcase themselves as an example of perfection.
  These  highly curated caricatures of influencers are exactly what children are most fond of as it makes an unobtainable level of perfection appear achievable to them.
  Insights into the life of influencers have been inspiring to countless children across the world, and the lost pursuit of perfection can have an adverse effect on the mental health of young people.
  In the UK, it was recently revealed that almost  one-in-five children want to become a social media influencer themselves when they grow up.
  Children, and especially teenagers, can develop a sense of identity when they compare themselves with their peers. This natural process can become distorted when they begin to make similar comparisons online with filtered images on social networks.
  According to  a recent study published in Pediatrics, children who view images of influencers with unhealthy snacks are more likely to consume unhealthy foods as a result.
  One of the largest problems with influencer culture stems from how aspirational the perception of an influencer can be. Children are finding motivation in the fame and brand affiliations of influencers online and many are actively attempting to build their own profiles online in a bid to reach similar levels of popularity.
  In attempting, and potentially failing on such a huge stage, the results could be extremely bad for the child’s self-esteem.
  There are many children today who actively engage in influencer behaviour online. They continually look to create and post content in order to amass more likes and shares on online social platforms. This directly affects the self-esteem of teens and young adults today, who can now measure their success on quantifiable factors like comments and the virality of their posts online.
  This can carry harmful consequences due to the superficiality of the life that they’re trying to achieve in their bid for influencer-levels of popularity.
   Away from the lost appeal of the influencer in the eyes of impressionable young people, and the dire psychological effects they can have, there’s also a big problem emerging in the type of content that influential users post online.
  Influencers are largely unregulated in the type of content they produce. Where, in the past, young people found appeal in television personalities and artists, they would only gain insights into their behaviour in the media and various publications that are typically well-regulated by governing bodies to avoid the condoning of the actions they report.
  Today, influencers have a direct avenue of communication with young people - meaning they can preach directly to them without any knowledge of the damage they’re causing or regulator on hand to stop their content from being aired.
  As a consequence, influencers have run into plenty of controversies regarding promoting  gambling to children,  engaging in explicit trolling,  condoning eating disorders, and encouraging  extreme beauty treatments.
  In a notable recent example of the dangers that social media users with influence hold to impressionable viewers, one TikTok user recorded herself using cotton wool buds to rub  hydrogen peroxide on her teeth in order to make them appear whiter.
  In a video that amassed over 15 million views, the user even said during filming “if you’re a dentist, don’t tell me this is wrong.” Subsequently, users who viewed the video opted to undertake the hazardous DIY treatment themselves and post the results.
  The video has caused concerned dentists to speak out. Talking to Health.com, Chris Strandberg said: “Prolonged bleaching with these high concentrations of hydrogen peroxide, especially when used multiple days in a row, can lead to highly irritated gums and sensitive teeth.
  Gum irritation can get severe if more bleaching is done when the gums are already irritated. Tooth sensitivity is usually temporary (24 hours or so), but significant bleaching can increase tooth sensitivity permanently with long term use.”
  The fact that social media users are advocating extreme and illegal teeth whitening measures rather than the use of approved products illustrates the eagerness that young people have in attempting to replicate the levels of perfection that are prevalent on Instagram and other networks.
   With the recent rise of TikTok adding to an already burgeoning list of networks it’s fair to say that social media and the influencers that have immersed themselves online aren’t going anywhere soon.
  While this may not seem like a good thing in light of this article, it’s important to state that many influencers create safe spaces for young people to build a sense of community and friendship with like-minded followers.
  If you’re concerned about the negative effects that influencers may have on your children in terms of affiliate marketing and encouraging unhealthy behavior, it’s worth starting up a dialogue with your kids.
  By asking them simple questions about what’s really happening when an influencer markets a product, it can help create a strong impact.
  Ask your children what the influencer is trying to achieve by showcasing a product on their account - was it attempting to make audiences laugh?
    Question whether the influencer has presented all the information necessary to accompany the product, and ask how the adverts make your children feel.
  The same approach should be taken for hazardous beauty tips and the condoning of unhealthy behaviour. With a vast and expanding online social ecosystem, it’s difficult to uphold any form of regulation, but by helping young people to become more aware of the content they’re seeing, it’s possible to make life on social networks a much safer place for the future.

title: 没有规则的榜样：影响者对儿童有害吗？
title_en: Role Models Without Regulation: Are Influencers Damaging for Children?

HTC Vive正在进一步投资于他们的业务开发生态系统；他们刚刚推出了专注于业务的VR应用套件。
新的Vive XR Suite是由HTC及其合作伙伴开发的五款面向企业的应用程序捆绑在一起，使从员工到学生的任何人都可以在家中工作时保持专注和投入。
以下是这些应用程序的列表，它们做什么，以及HTC在开发这些应用程序时与谁合作。
·万岁校园(Vive Campus)：这款应用程序模拟与VirBELA合作开发的远程员工的办公环境。
·VIVE SESSION：这是一款专注于教育的应用程序，可以让讲座在VR中呈现，这是我与Engage合作开发的。
·社交万岁(Vive Social)：这款应用是与VRChat合作开发的，工作方式与VRChat类似，为员工或学生提供社交空间。
·万岁博物馆(Vive Museum)：这可能是最有趣的应用程序，它是一个虚拟美术馆和博物馆，它是与其他现实博物馆(Museum Of Other Reality)合作开发的。
每个应用程序都是为支持非VR用户而开发的，它们都可以在PC上使用，有些应用程序，比如vive Sync，也可以在智能手机上使用。
在宣布推出HTC的声明中，HTC首席执行官王雪红(Cher Wang)表示：“应用技术解决现实世界的问题一直是我们HTC哲学的核心宗旨。Vive XR Suite提供终极VR企业软件解决方案，专为帮助企业以安全和创新的方式解决当今最紧迫的问题而设计。通过将HTC的资源放在这些应用程序的背后，我们可以确保世界各地的企业客户获得他们所需的系统可扩展性和专业支持。“。
这些应用程序是单独开发的，但它们都可以在大多数PC VR头盔上通过一次登录访问，但HTC已经与Pico、惠普和NReal等公司合作，在他们的头盔上预装该套件。
全套的“Vive XR Suite”一个月每个座位收费30美元，全年每个座位250美元。
note_en:
HTC Vive is investing further in their business development ecosystem; they have just  launched their business focused suite of VR apps.
  The new VIVE XR Suite is a bundle of five enterprise focused apps developed by HTC and their partners that allows anyone from employees to students stay focused and engaged while working from home.
  What follows is a list of the apps, what they do, and who HTC have partnered with in their development.
  · VIVE Campus: This app simulates an office environment for remote workers, developed in partnership with  VirBELA.
 · VIVE Session: This is an education focused app that allows lectures to be presented in VR, developed I partnership with  ENGAGE.
 · VIVE Social: This app was developed in partnership with  VRChat and works in a similar way by providing employees or student with a social space.
 · VIVE Museum: This is perhaps the most interesting app, it is a virtual art gallery and museum, it was developed in partnership with  Museum of Other Realities.
 Each of the apps have been developed to support non-VR users, they can all be used on PC and some, like VIVE Sync, can also be used on smartphones.
  In a statement announcing the launch the CEO of HTC, Cher Wang, said,  “Applying technology to solve real-world problems has always been a core tenet of our philosophy at HTC. The Vive XR Suite offers the ultimate VR enterprise software solution, specifically designed to help businesses solve today’s most pressing problems in a secure and innovative way By putting the resources of HTC behind these apps, we can ensure business customers around the world receive the system scalability and professional support they demand.”
 The apps were developed separately but they can all be accessed through a single login on most PC VR headsets, but HTC have partnered with companies like Pico, HP, and NReal to preload the suite on their headsets.
 The full “Vive XR Suite” will cost companies $30 per seat for one month or $250 per seat for a full year.
title: HTC推出“vive XR Suite”，这是一款旨在提高远程生产力的VR应用

note: 几周前，我有了一个想法，就是直接从Dazzler的FPGA的引脚上运行一台6502。Dazzler有27个完全未提交的IO，因此将8位微处理器作为一个大脑在大桶中运行应该是直截了当的--CPU是真实的，但系统的其余部分是由FPGA模拟的。连接非常简单--来自65C02的27个信号直接进入FPGA。大约一周后，木板又回来了。有一个适用于Dazzler的插座和一个适用于65C02的插座。通用异步收发器(UART)和电源连接在左边。所有东西都插上电源了：电源、SparkFun UART、Dazzler、HDMI线缆，最后是65C02。一切都准备好了！但是，作为第一个测试，应该运行什么呢？当然，我不需要编写6502程序，编写RAM模拟器，然后对其进行调试？当然不会--这篇文章解释说，如果你把0xEA放在6502的数据总线上，它会很方便地永远在内存中循环。这很简单。0xEA是NOP的操作码，因此CPU会看到所有内存都被NOP指令填满。因此，它会在执行NOP的所有64K字节地址空间中循环。当程序计数器到达地址0xFFFF时，它绕回到0x0000...。无限循环。这一切看起来像什么？这是几分钟的工作，修改了Dazzler本身的波形显示，显示了所有连接的6502信号：这是复位后的头几个时钟。首先，6502从地址0xFFFC和0xFFFD获取引导地址。当然，此16位地址是0xEAEA，因此它跳转到地址0xEAEA并查找...。操作码0xEA(NOP)。这需要两个时钟来执行，并且地址总线递增到0xEAEB。诸若此类。您可以看到地址总线在屏幕右下角开始递增，每两个时钟递增一次，因为NOP是6502上的2周期指令。这是一个很好的开始--看起来65C02正在愉快地执行代码。下一步是将一个小RAM与一个小程序连接起来。未来几周会有更多关于这方面的信息，请订阅以关注进展。谢谢你的阅读。Dazzler即将进入最后一周的人群供应。如果你已经支持了，非常感谢。
note_en: A couple of weeks ago I had a idea of running a 6502 directly off the pins of the  Dazzler's FPGA. The Dazzler has 27 completely uncommitted IOs, so it should be straightforward to run the 8-bit microprocessor as a "brain in a vat" -- the CPU is real but the rest of the system is being simulated by the FPGA. Hookup is very straightforward -- 27 signals from the 65C02 go straight into the FPGA.  The boards came back about a week later. There's a socket for the Dazzler, and a socket for the 65C02.  UART and power connections are on the left.  Here's everything plugged in: power,  Sparkfun UART, Dazzler, HDMI cable and finally the 65C02. All is ready!    But what to run as a first test? Surely I don't have to write a 6502 program, write the RAM simulator, and debug it? Of course not -  this article explains that if you place 0xEA on the 6502's data bus, it will handily loop forever around memory. It's quite simple. 0xEA is the opcode for NOP, so CPU sees all of memory filled with NOP instructions. It cycles around all of its 64 Kbyte address space executing the NOPs. When the program counter reaches address 0xFFFF, it wraps around to 0x0000 ... infinite loop.  What does all this look like? It was the work of a few minutes to hack up a waveform display on the Dazzler itself, showing all the connected 6502 signals:    This is the first few clocks after reset. First the 6502 fetches the boot address from addresses 0xFFFC and 0xFFFD. This 16-bit address is 0xEAEA, of course, so it jumps to address 0xEAEA and finds... opcode 0xEA (NOP). That takes two clocks to execute, and the address bus increments to 0xEAEB. And so on. You can see the address bus starting to increment at the bottom-right of the screen, once every two clocks because NOP is a 2-cycle instruction on the 6502.  That's a good start -- looks like the 65C02 is happily executing code. The next step is to hook up a small RAM with a tiny program. More on this in coming weeks,  subscribe to follow the progress.  Thanks for reading.  Dazzler is coming into its last week on Crowd Supply. Thanks very much if you already backed it.
title: 微处理器只想要一样东西，而且是0xEA
title_en: Microprocessor only wants one thing and it's 0xEA

note:
Trickbot是一家出租僵尸网络，自2016年以来已经感染了100多万台设备。在微软和一系列行业合作伙伴上周采取协调行动扰乱Trickbot之后，Trickbot的运营商正在寻找新的方式来维持运营。
在周二发布的最新消息中，微软安全与信托公司副总裁汤姆·伯特(Tom Burt)表示，此次行动最初设法拿下了Trickbot用来控制其庞大的受感染设备网络的69台服务器中的62台。Trickbot运营商的回应是迅速启动了59台新服务器，微软得以淘汰了所有服务器，只有一台除外。
总体而言，全行业的行动已经拿下了128台被确认属于Trickbot的服务器中的120台。现在，Trickbot的回应是利用一个相互竞争的犯罪集团来分发Trickbot恶意软件。
伯特写道：“这是众多迹象之一，向我们表明，面对不断受到攻击的关键基础设施，Trickbot运营商正在争先恐后地寻找其他方式来保持活跃。”“虽然与其他参与者的安排不会使Trickbot能够与其本土能力相提并论，但这也提醒人们，维护网络空间安全面临许多威胁，对人们-特别是那些参与我们选举过程安全的人-保持警惕是很重要的。”
伯特过去曾监督过几次全球僵尸网络被攻破，他说，该行业在这方面正在变得更好。在确定新的Trickbot服务器后，微软及其合作伙伴在短短三个小时内就找到了各自的主机提供商，启动了必要的法律行动，并关闭了新的基础设施。在许多合作伙伴的协调下，从通知托管服务器的提供商开始，一次关闭所需的时间不到6分钟。
伯特还说，重建命令服务器的基础设施是很耗时的，而且不是简单地设置新服务器的问题。需要配置新服务器才能开始与僵尸网络受感染的设备对话并发出命令，所有这些都需要时间。他说，许多仍然存在的服务器是路由器或其他类型的物联网设备，它们不容易受到正常关闭程序的影响。
note_en:
Operators of Trickbot—a for-hire botnet that has infected more than 1 million devices since 2016—are looking for new ways to stay afloat after Microsoft and a host of industry partners  took coordinated action to disrupt it last week.
 In an  update published on Tuesday, Microsoft Corporate VP for Security & Trust Tom Burt said the operation initially managed to take down 62 of the 69 servers Trickbot was known to be using to control its vast network of infected devices. Trickbot operators responded by quickly spinning up 59 new servers, and Microsoft was able to eliminate all of them except for one.
 In all, the industrywide operation has taken down 120 of 128 servers identified as belonging to Trickbot. Now, Trickbot is responding by using a competing criminal group to distribute the Trickbot malware.
  “This is one of many signs that suggests to us that, faced with its critical infrastructure under repeated attack, Trickbot operators are scrambling to find other ways to stay active,” Burt wrote. “While an arrangement with other actors will not enable Trickbot to equal its homegrown capabilities, it’s also a reminder that there are many threats to keeping cyberspace secure and it’s important for people—especially those involved in the security of our electoral processes—to stay vigilant.”
 Burt, who has overseen several global botnet takedowns in the past, said the industry is getting better at them. After identifying new Trickbot servers, Microsoft and its partners have been able to locate their respective hosting providers, initiated required legal actions, and taken down the new infrastructure in as little as three hours. With the coordination of the many partners, one takedown took less than six minutes from the time the provider hosting the server was notified.
 Burt also said that rebuilding an infrastructure of command servers is time-consuming and isn’t simply a matter of setting up new servers. “New servers need to be provisioned to begin talking with the botnet’s infected devices and issuing commands, all of which takes time.” He said that many of the servers that remain standing are routers or other types of Internet-of-things devices that aren’t vulnerable to normal takedown procedures.
title: Trickbot-微软攻击的雇佣僵尸网络-正在争先恐后地生存
title_en: Trickbot—the for-hire botnet Microsoft attacked—is scrambling to stay alive
